entries.title: CSS Container Query Match Visualizer
entries.notes: Container queries are under-tooled—most devs rely on browser DevTools which lack a dedicated panel. A sidebar showing active containment contexts, computed query values, and breakpoint triggers would fill a clear gap. Buildable by parsing CSSOM and MutationObservers.
entries.pain: Debugging which container queries match and why is invisible without inspecting computed values manually

entries.title: Viewport Unit Live Preview Panel
entries.notes: Mobile browsers handle viewport units differently and mobile UI chrome changes the visible area. A live overlay showing exact computed viewport values for all unit types would eliminate back-and-forth. Simple DOM measurement API calls, no backend needed.
entries.pain: Testing vh, svh, dvh, vw units across mobile browsers requires constant resizing and guessing

entries.title: Cross-Origin PostMessage Relay Hub
entries.notes: postMessage is widely used for cross-origin communication but debugging tools are primitive. A structured event log with filtering, replay, and payload diffing would accelerate iframe-heavy app development.
entries.pain: Inspecting and replaying postMessage events between iframes requires filtering console noise

entries.title: Virtual WebAuthn Authenticator
entries.notes: Passkey adoption is accelerating but local testing remains painful. A browser-native virtual authenticator that simulates credential creation and assertion would let devs test WebAuthn flows without physical tokens.
entries.pain: Testing passkey flows locally requires hardware security keys or emulators that are clunky

entries.title: Sales Prospect Stack Commitment Detector
entries.notes: Existing intent tools track firmographics but miss technology hiring signals. Scraping job postings for stack-specific roles and flagging competitors fills a prospecting gap. Feeds into existing CRMs via API.
entries.pain: Outreach personalization wasted when prospect engineering team already committed to a competitor's platform

entries.title: Real-Time Accessibility Tree Sidebar
entries.notes: The accessibility tree is the canonical source for screen readers but it's opaque. A sidebar rendering the computed tree alongside the DOM with ARIA property annotations would make a11y bugs obvious during development.
entries.pain: Accessibility bugs slip through because computing the accessibility tree from DOM requires imagination

entries.title: ContentEditable DOM Serializer Debugger
entries.notes: Every WYSIWYG editor struggles with contenteditable quirks. A debug panel showing the actual serialized DOM, command stack, and mutation diff would help developers understand and fix formatting issues faster.
entries.pain: Rich text editors built on contenteditable produce mangled DOM that is hard to reverse-engineer

entries.title: WebRTC Connection Stats Dashboard
entries.notes: WebRTC exposes rich connection statistics but they're buried in chrome://webrtc-internals. A real-time dashboard surfacing packet loss, jitter, bandwidth estimates, and codec info would make debugging accessible to more developers.
entries.pain: Diagnosing WebRTC audio/video quality issues requires manually collecting and interpreting RTCPeerConnection stats

entries.title: Fingerprint Surface Area Tracker
entries.notes: Fingerprinting vectors are proliferating but users have no visibility. A cumulative tracker showing canvas hash, WebGL renderer, audio fingerprint, font list, and composite entropy score across visited sites would raise awareness.
entries.pain: Understanding how uniquely identifiable your browser is across sites requires piecing together signals manually

entries.title: OpenAPI Contract Conflict Analyzer
entries.notes: Schema validation catches type mismatches but not semantic conflicts like changing enum meanings or removing behavior. Comparing provider and consumer OpenAPI specs and surfacing conflict heatmaps would prevent silent failures.
entries.pain: API breaking changes silently break integrations because schema validation misses semantic conflicts

entries.title: Design Token Semantic Diff Viewer
entries.notes: Tokens stored in Figma or code get diffed but semantic meaning is lost. A visual diff highlighting renamed tokens, deprecated values, and semantic shifts (e.g., color purpose change) would make token PRs reviewable.
entries.pain: Reviewing design token changes in PRs is painful because JSON/YAML diffs don't show semantic impact

entries.title: Gong Call Commitment Calendar Linker
entries.notes: Gong captures commitments but follow-up lives in separate systems. A flow detecting commitment language in transcripts and prompting calendar task creation would close the loop between insight and action.
entries.pain: Sales reps lose track of commitments made on calls and fail to follow through, damaging trust

entries.title: CSS Containment Boundary Visualizer
entries.notes: CSS containment is powerful for performance but its effects are opaque. An overlay highlighting containment boundaries, isolation scopes, and layout responsibility would help devs understand and optimize rendering costs.
entries.pain: Understanding which elements have containment applied and what layout scope they create is invisible

entries.title: Lead Company News Timeline Aggregator
entries.notes: Intent data platforms exist but are expensive and focus on firmographics. A lightweight scraper aggregating a company's news, LinkedIn posts, and job postings into a timeline would surface technology adoption signals at a fraction of the cost.
entries.pain: Sales reps manually research prospects and miss signals because aggregating news and job posts is time-consuming

entries.title: AI Citation Source Verifier
entries.notes: LLM hallucinations are a major trust blocker. A tool that fetches cited URLs and checks whether the cited content actually supports the claim—surfacing confidence mismatches—would be broadly valuable as AI-assisted writing spreads.
entries.pain: Verifying that LLM citations actually support the claims requires manually cross-checking each source
